clinical_coherence.notes: 68M East Asian with splenectomy, DM2, gout, partially treated with oral amoxicillin. Clinical picture is coherent. Age >60 adds appropriate complexity regarding antibiotic coverage (Listeria). However, this is the third moderate case featuring an asplenic patient on oral amoxicillin with very similar CSF profiles (WBC 348, 72% neutrophils). The template repetition is notable.

tool_output_consistency.notes: CSF WBC 348, 72% neutrophils, protein 152.5, glucose 34.9. Consistent with partially treated meningitis. Similar to M01 and M02 but slightly lower WBC reflecting age-related immune response variation. All tool outputs concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G00.1 correct. Appropriately identifies need for Listeria coverage given age >60. Critical actions and contraindicated actions are clinically sound.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Appropriately moderate. Age >60 with partial treatment, asplenia, and gout medication considerations add complexity.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 68M East Asian male with remote splenectomy and outdated vaccination is realistic. Gout with allopurinol adds a common comorbidity without overcomplicating.

educational_value.notes: The age-based Listeria coverage point is important, but the case is the third nearly identical partially-treated-asplenic-meningitis template. Diminishing returns on educational value within the set. The gout and age-related considerations add some incremental value.

issues_found: Third moderate case with asplenic patient on oral amoxicillin and nearly identical CSF profile (WBC 348, 72% neutrophils) -- template repetition significantly reduces diversity
CSF neutrophil percentage (72%) is identical across all three moderate cases
strengths: Age >60 correctly triggers Listeria coverage discussion
East Asian demographic adds diversity to the set
Outdated pneumococcal vaccination (12 years ago) is a realistic detail
